commit.message:
fix: multi-episode files, Season 00 support, duplicate episode guard

controller/controller.py

Multi-episode filenames (S01E01E02.mkv):
- Replaced _EPISODE_PATTERNS list with four named class-level compiled
  regexes: _RE_SxxEyy (chained episodes), _RE_NxNN (1x01), _RE_EPISODE_N
  (Episode N), _RE_E_SPLIT (splits E-token block into individual numbers).
- Added _episodes_from_filename(fname, season) → set[int]: extracts ALL
  episode numbers from one filename. S01E01E02E03 returns {1, 2, 3}.
  Season token mismatch still blocks fall-through to looser patterns.
- _scan_episode_files() now delegates to _episodes_from_filename() via
  set-union so every episode in a combined file is registered. Previously
  only the first E-token was captured, causing false gap detection.

Season 00 / Specials:
- _scan_library_folder() now recognises "Season 00" and "Specials[/]"
  directories and maps them to season 0 (Jellyfin convention).
- A log line is emitted when season 0 is detected so it is never silently
  ignored.

Season UX improvement:
- Season prompt default value is now set to the highest season present in
  library_state when no resume metadata exists, reducing the number of
  keystrokes for the common "continuing an in-progress season" case.
- Hint text upgraded from "S01  S02" to "S01:8ep  S02:3ep" so the user
  can see at a glance which season is incomplete.

Duplicate episode guard:
- After the user confirms episode numbers, _scan_episode_files() is called
  once more on the destination folder. Any episode number that already has
  a file on disk triggers a visible WARNING log line and a yes/no confirm
  dialog before proceeding. Files are never silently overwritten
  (unique_path() handles the rename), but the user is explicitly notified.

tests/test_behavior_guards.py
- test_multi_episode_file_contributes_both_episodes
- test_multi_episode_file_three_episodes
- test_multi_episode_file_does_not_report_gap_for_covered_range
- test_multi_episode_ignores_wrong_season
- test_scan_library_folder_detects_season_00
- test_scan_library_folder_detects_specials_folder
- test_scan_library_folder_season_00_logs_detection
- test_episodes_from_filename_single
- test_episodes_from_filename_wrong_season_returns_empty

commit.message:
Add relay=False option to hide upstream signals from downstream

When relay=False on add_tcp()/add_serial():
- Upstream signals are discovered and decoded hub-side
- Signals are NOT exposed to Loggbok (no B0/B5/D1 frames)
- Command forwarding (ACTIVATE/DEACTIVATE/WRITE_DATA) skips
  non-relayed upstreams
- Disconnect notification (STATUS_UPSTREAM_LOST) skips
  non-relayed upstreams
- Slave IDs stay contiguous for relayed upstreams

Access non-relayed signal values via:
  arduino = hub.add_serial('COM3', 9600, 'Arduino', relay=False)
  arduino.signals['temperature'].value
  hub['Arduino']['temperature'].value

Both add_tcp() and add_serial() now return the upstream handle
(works for relay=True too).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
A-1 Transactional Outbox + A-3 InsightGenerator Sidekiq Batch + audit fixes (#228)

* Initial plan

* A-1: Remove redundant AlertNotificationWorker.perform_async from AlertDispatchService and GatewayTelemetryWorker

EwsAlert.after_create_commit :dispatch_notifications! already safely enqueues
the notification worker after transaction commit. The explicit calls in:
- AlertDispatchService.create_and_dispatch_alert!
- AlertDispatchService.create_fraud_alert!
- GatewayTelemetryWorker (post-transaction)
were redundant (double enqueue) and unsafe when called within transaction
context (phantom Sidekiq jobs on rollback).

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Alexey-Lukin/silken_net/sessions/c9a85226-db63-4f33-977f-651f621c83fc

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* A-3: InsightGeneratorService → Sidekiq::Batch orchestration

Split monolithic InsightGeneratorService#perform into batch processing:
- InsightGeneratorOrchestratorWorker: creates Sidekiq::Batch, chunks clusters
- GenerateClusterInsightWorker: processes ~100 clusters per worker
- InsightBatchCallbacks: chains ClusterHealthCheckWorker + cleanup on success
- DailyAggregationWorker: uses orchestrator instead of sync service call
- InsightGeneratorService: added process_cluster_batch() and cluster_baselines()
  public methods; existing perform() preserved for backward compatibility

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Alexey-Lukin/silken_net/sessions/c9a85226-db63-4f33-977f-651f621c83fc

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cleanup_old_logs! to preserve TelemetryLog with oracle_status=dispatched

Logs awaiting Chainlink oracle callback (oracle_status='dispatched') must not
be deleted during cleanup — OracleCallbacksController looks them up by
chainlink_request_id. Deleting them breaks the Proof of Growth minting pipeline
(RecordNotFound → 5 wasted retries → tokens never minted).

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Alexey-Lukin/silken_net/sessions/c9a85226-db63-4f33-977f-651f621c83fc

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Audit fixes: remove race condition in orchestrator, clean up dead code, add missing specs

- Remove global AiInsight.delete_all from InsightGeneratorOrchestratorWorker
  to prevent race condition with parallel child workers. Per-cluster
  idempotency in GenerateClusterInsightWorker is sufficient.
- Remove dead _ = pending_alert_id assignment in GatewayTelemetryWorker;
  check_system_health no longer returns alert.id (notifications handled
  by EwsAlert.after_create_commit callback).
- Add .cleanup_old_logs! class method specs (standalone, dispatched guard,
  recent log preservation).
- Add fraud detection spec for GenerateClusterInsightWorker batch mode.
- Add orchestrator idempotency spec confirming no global delete_all.
- Document DailyAggregationWorker edge case (inactive trees with telemetry).
